compiled_state.title: dso:code-reviewer-standard does not write reviewer-findings.json to orchestrator-specified path
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Observed Behavior

When dso:code-reviewer-standard is dispatched with explicit instructions to write findings to a specific path (e.g., `/tmp/review-93bd-final/reviewer-findings.json`), the agent returns REVIEWER_HASH and REVIEW_RESULT in its output but does NOT write the file to the specified path. The file is absent when record-review.sh attempts to read it.

## Steps to Reproduce

Dispatch dso:code-reviewer-standard with prompt including:

```
Write findings to `/tmp/review-93bd-final/reviewer-findings.json` with diff hash `<hash>`
```

After agent returns, run:
```
bash .claude/scripts/dso record-review.sh --reviewer-hash <HASH> --expected-hash <HASH> --findings-file /tmp/review-93bd-final/reviewer-findings.json
```

Result: ERROR: reviewer-findings.json not found

## Prompt Given to Agent

```
Review the staged diff for task `93bd-7940` (tag policy documentation + design-approve.sh refactor + RED marker fix for generate-claude-md).

**Diff hash**: `04bac43fbbc72a8287269a517540749ac7d51bc875f06adc495f3a3d28053763`
**Artifacts dir**: `/tmp/workflow-plugin-8023e4870608c1c6`

[...context about staged files and test results...]

Write findings to `/tmp/workflow-plugin-8023e4870608c1c6/reviewer-findings.json` with diff hash `04bac43fbbc72a8287269a517540749ac7d51bc875f06adc495f3a3d28053763`.
```

## Likely Cause

The reviewer agent uses write-reviewer-findings.sh which computes its own ARTIFACTS_DIR from REPO_ROOT hash. When the orchestrator specifies a custom path, the agent ignores it and writes to its own computed path (e.g., /tmp/workflow-plugin-<different-hash>/reviewer-findings.json). The orchestrator's artifacts dir and the agent's computed dir differ when the agent runs in a worktree with a different REPO_ROOT.

## Expected Behavior

The agent should write reviewer-findings.json to the path explicitly specified in the prompt, OR the record-review.sh --findings-file flag should accept the agent's computed path via some discovery mechanism.

## Workaround

Used a fresh artifacts dir (/tmp/review-93bd-final) with a new dispatch. Same issue recurred. Required multiple review dispatch iterations until the file was found at the orchestrator's path.

## Impact

Blocks cherry-pick commit workflow — requires repeated review dispatches to work around.

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode): bug targets an LLM-behavioral skill/agent/prompt file. Per CLAUDE.md rule 22 and behavioral-testing-standard Rule 5, any modification requires dedicated bot-psychologist investigation and explicit user approval. DEFERRED: batch-escalating to end-of-session user review; no code change applied this session.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: USER_APPROVAL (2026-04-20): User directive — 'Fixes recommended by bot-psychologist are approved' for all Category D intent-confirmed clusters. Cluster B fixes approved for application: (a) reviewer-base.md Step 3 quote _OUTPUT_FLAG, (b) write-reviewer-findings.sh REVIEWER_ARTIFACTS_DIR override. Build-review-agents.sh regen required after reviewer-base.md edits.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation (cluster B, sonnet, KERNEL): Taxonomy #5 Multi-File State De-sync + #16 Instruction Locality. ROOT_CAUSE: write-reviewer-findings.sh calls get_artifacts_dir which hashes REPO_ROOT. Under worktree dispatch, sub-agent CWD is orchestrator tree, so git rev-parse returns wrong REPO_ROOT, produces different hash, writes to /tmp/workflow-plugin-WRONG/. FINDINGS_OUTPUT escape hatch is unreliable because not always injected and unquoted _OUTPUT_FLAG variable breaks paths with special chars. PROPOSED_FIXES: (a) reviewer-base.md Step 3 quote the _OUTPUT_FLAG expansion so --output argument survives paths with quotes or spaces. (b) write-reviewer-findings.sh add env-var escape hatch: check REVIEWER_ARTIFACTS_DIR before get_artifacts_dir resolution, letting orchestrator pin write path directly. Confidence: high. Minimal-fix applied: true.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed
comments.author: Worktree

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — ticket references agent not writing to specified path (behavior drift); affected file agents/code-reviewer-standard.md
comments.author: Worktree
